description: This table records the enrollment details of users in various courses, including their progress and completion status.

columns.name: enrollment_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each enrollment record.

columns.name: user_id
columns.description: Identifier for the user enrolled in the course.

columns.name: course_id
columns.description: Identifier for the course in which the user is enrolled.

columns.name: enrolled_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the user enrolled in the course.

columns.name: completed_at
columns.description: Timestamp when the user completed the course.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Current status of the enrollment (e.g., enrolled, in_progress, completed, dropped).

columns.name: progress_percent
columns.description: Percentage of course progress made by the user.

columns.name: final_grade
columns.description: Final grade received by the user for the course.

grain: One row represents a single enrollment of a user in a course.
data_quality_concerns: user_id and course_id may be null, leading to orphaned records.
progress_percent and final_grade should be validated to ensure they are within expected ranges (0-100).
recommended_transformations: Implement foreign key constraints for user_id and course_id to ensure referential integrity.
Add checks to ensure progress_percent and final_grade are between 0 and 100.
Consider adding default values for status to avoid null values.
